fatty liver: Fatty liver (Steatosis) is the buildup of excess fat in the liver cells. Grade I is mild.
calculus: Calculus is the medical term for a stone (like a kidney stone or gallstone).
echotexture: Echotexture refers to how the tissue looks on ultrasound. Normal echotexture means the organ tissue is healthy.
distended: Distended means swollen or full (usually normal for a bladder before a scan).
hydronephrosis: Hydronephrosis is swelling of the kidney due to a build-up of urine.
lymphadenopathy: Lymphadenopathy means lymph nodes are enlarged/swollen, often due to infection.
ascites: Ascites is fluid collection in the abdomen, often a sign of liver disease.
